---
title: "Schedule"
toc: true
---

> **Public version.** A generic week-by-week topic map. Specific
> calendar dates, conference sign-ups, and submission links live in
> Blackboard for each section.

This course is a **conference-based studio**. Scheduled Mon/Wed class
times are **not** ordinary required lectures. They are protected time
for the five **required conferences**, plus optional demonstrations,
open studio work, troubleshooting, and individual help. The pacing
mechanism for the course is the **weekly deliverable**.

**Required attendance** is tied to the **five conference weeks** —
Weeks 1, 4, 7, 10, and 13. Every other scheduled meeting is optional
support time you may attend for help or work alongside others.

## Week-by-week

| Wk | Topic | Studio focus | Deliverable | Conference |
|----|-------|--------------|-------------|-----------|
| 1 | Setup, Quarto, portfolio folder | Install R, RStudio, TinyTeX, Quarto; first render; portfolio layout | HW 1 — first Quarto document | **Setup conference (required)** |
| 2 | LaTeX foundations | Sectioning, math environments, first math document | HW 2 — math typesetting writeup | — |
| 3 | Math environments; theorem and proof | `amsmath` / `amsthm`; aligned equations; proof blocks | HW 3 — proofs and equations | — |
| 4 | Figures, tables, bibliography | `graphicx`, tables, `biblatex` / `natbib` workflow | HW 4 — figures and bibliography | **LaTeX checkpoint conference (required)** |
| 5 | LaTeX Project draft week | Paper pick, scope, one-page outline | HW 5 — paper pick + outline | — |
| 6 | LaTeX Project | Studio time for the project | **LaTeX Project due** | — |
| 7 | R foundations | Vectors, data frames, `dplyr` basics | HW 7 — first R script | **R transition conference (required)** |
| 8 | Visualization with `ggplot2` | Geoms, faceting, themes, labels | HW 8 — visual data story | — |
| 9 | Simulation and reproducibility | `set.seed()`, replicate, CLT ideas | HW 9 — simulation report | — |
| 10 | R Project | Studio time for the project | **R Project due** | **R Project conference (required)** |
| 11 | AI module I — debugging and verification | Prompting, scoping, verifying, citation hygiene | HW 11 — AI debugging audit | — |
| 12 | AI module II — drafting and critique | Drafting prose, catching hallucinations, critique | HW 12 — AI Use Reflection | — |
| 13 | Workflow bridge / second-tool exploration | Light Sage / Python / Octave / CAS exploration; portfolio polish | HW 13 — bridge mini-report | **Portfolio-workflow conference (required)** |
| 14 | Portfolio assembly | Optional open studio and final-polish support | Portfolio draft | — |
| 15 | Final polish and submission | Optional open studio | **Final portfolio + 1–2 page reflection due** | — |

**Eleven weekly labs/homework** (HW 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13).
The **best 9 of 11** count for ordinary weekly-work credit.
**HW 11 and HW 12 are the dedicated AI module assignments and are not
droppable.** Projects, required conferences, AI verification
components, and the final portfolio are also **not** droppable.

## How to use this schedule

- **Read by row.** Each row gives the week's topic, what the scheduled
  studio time focuses on if you attend, what is due that week, and
  whether the week includes a required conference.
- **Most weeks have a weekly deliverable.** That is the actual pacing
  mechanism for the course, regardless of whether you come to a
  scheduled meeting.
- **Dates live in Blackboard.** This site only shows "Week 1, Week 2,
  …" so it stays useful across terms. Blackboard is also the
  authoritative source for conference sign-ups and section-specific
  due dates.

For the assignment briefs themselves, see
[assignments/](assignments/index.qmd).
